mnemonics: FRUC-MESC (Functional, Reliable, Usable, Efficient, Maintainable, Portable, Secure, Correct)
keyPoints: Software quality is defined by its conformance to explicit functional requirements and implicit stakeholder expectations.
Key quality attributes include reliability, usability, efficiency, maintainability, and portability.
Quality is measured through metrics like defect density, mean time to failure (MTTF), and customer satisfaction.
commonMistakes: Equating the absence of bugs as the sole measure of quality, ignoring attributes like usability and maintainability.
Focusing only on internal technical quality (e.g., code quality) while neglecting external user-centric quality attributes.
